economic_notes: O'Chiese operates 14 government business enterprises including energy, safety services, contracting, well services, gaming, land holdings, communications, cascade power, hospitality, marketplace, land development, utilities, and business/investment centre. GBEs had combined revenue of $60,716,341 and net income of $996,050 in 2024. Royalty income from Ottawa Trust was $24,033,827 (down significantly from $63,979,792 in 2023). Settlement Trust exists but is excluded from consolidated statements.
notes: Audit is QUALIFIED on two bases: (1) not all asset retirement obligations have been correctly estimated and recorded, and (2) auditors were unable to obtain sufficient audit evidence for certain transactions including expenses, payroll and other income. Long-term debt of $57,188,192 includes CMHC mortgages ($2,959,910), TD loans ($12,714,245), and FNFA promissory note ($41,389,554 net of debt reserve fund). Federal trust funds (Ottawa Trust) of $91,875,266 represent a significant asset. The O'Chiese Settlement Trust (from 2007 Canada settlement) is excluded from consolidation. Royalty income dropped dramatically from $63.9M in 2023 to $24.0M in 2024. Annual debt service calculated as principal repayments on long-term debt ($899,365) plus promissory note repayments ($1,231,739). Segmented information note appears to be cut off in source document. total_government_transfers includes ISC ($27,617,173) + CMHC ($187,041) + HRD ($716,748) + FNDF ($666,921). OSR includes Ottawa Trust interest, royalties, equity in investments, and other income.
